title: stellaria media subsp. umbrosa
intro: Stellaria media subsp. umbrosa, also known as the lesser celandine or pilewort, is a perennial herb that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Stellaria media subsp. umbrosa include its ability to reduce swelling, ease pain, and combat infections, providing health benefits such as relief from colds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or poultice, and its leaves and roots are also used in folk medicine to create remedies for various ailments.
